problem: The middle of the film drags and the audience loses momentum.
prompt: How do you handle the slow second act in the edit?

options.label: Build a montage
options.description: Compress the development into a rhythmic sequence of short shots.

options.label: Trim scenes
options.description: Shorten existing scenes without changing their shape.

options.label: Leave it as is
options.description: Trust the material and keep the slower rhythm.

problem: A pivotal character reads as flat and their inner reaction is invisible.
prompt: How do you shoot the emotional turning point?

options.label: Go to a close-up
options.description: Hold tight on the face so the smallest reaction lands.

options.label: Keep the wide shot
options.description: Stay back and let the staging carry the moment.

options.label: Add a voiceover
options.description: Explain the feeling in narration instead of showing it.

problem: The lighting plan is too expensive for the budget and the schedule.
prompt: How do you light the location work?

options.label: Use natural light
options.description: Shoot with available light for realism and a lower cost.

options.label: Rent a full lighting rig
options.description: Buy total control at a cost the budget can barely carry.

options.label: Cut the lit scenes
options.description: Avoid the problem by removing the scenes entirely.
